title: aralia cordata var. continentalis
intro: Aralia cordata var. continentalis, also known as the Japanese angelica tree, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its large, lobed leaves and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The health benefits of Aralia cordata var. continentalis include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism and arthritis, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
